---
title: "Statistics by Intuition"
subtitle: "Lessons that build statistical thinking with modern, real-world examples"
---

## What this is

A growing collection of statistics lessons designed around one belief: **intuition
first, formalism second.** Every lesson starts with a real problem you might
actually care about — a medical test result, a spam filter, an election forecast —
and lets the math emerge from the problem rather than the other way around.

Each lesson includes:

- **A background primer** covering exactly the prerequisites you need — no more.
- **A structured lesson plan** with learning objectives, modules, and time estimates.
- **Worked modern examples** with real numbers you can check by hand.
- **Exercises and discussion prompts** to test whether the intuition stuck.

## Lessons

| # | Lesson | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | [Bayesian Statistics: Updating Beliefs with Evidence](lessons/01-bayesian-statistics/index.qmd) | Draft |

More lessons are planned. Each lives in its own directory under `lessons/` and
follows the same structure, so the collection stays navigable as it grows.

## How to use this site

Lessons are self-contained but ordered — later lessons may assume comfort with
earlier ones. Within a lesson, start with the lesson plan page, detour into the
background primer if anything feels shaky, then work through the examples page
with a pencil. The exercises are where the learning actually happens.
